message:
fix(daemon): harden status rendering, config resolution, and /proc scan against edge cases

cli_app.rs:
- Add guard in render_status() to skip pseudo-filesystems (squashfs,
  proc, sysfs, etc.) that report zero total capacity, preventing
  division-by-zero and nonsensical pressure readings in status output

core/config.rs:
- Add system-wide config fallback at /etc/sbh/config.toml: when no
  explicit --config path is given and the user-level config does not
  exist, check the system path before falling back to defaults — lets
  unprivileged `sbh status` invocations pick up the daemon's config
  without requiring --config; prints diagnostic to stderr when using
  system fallback

scanner/walker.rs:
- Add time and PID-count budgets to collect_open_files_linux(): a
  deadline (OPEN_FILES_SCAN_BUDGET) and pids_scanned counter (capped
  at OPEN_FILES_MAX_PIDS) cause early termination, preventing the
  daemon from hanging the system on machines with thousands of processes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
